instNote: ﻿Masses at 6 1/2, 8 1/2, and 10 1/2 A.M. every Sunday and Holy-day, at each of which instructions are generally given. Vespers on the same at 4 P.M. in Summer, and 3 1/2 P.M in Winter; after which a Sunday School is taught by a society of ladies and gentlemen of the congregation, at which between 150 and 200 children attend. Missions attended from this place, Darien (McIntosh Co.), Brunswick (Glynn Co.), St.Mary's (Camden CO.), and counties in the South-eastern part of the State, viz: Irwin, Clinch, Wilcox, &c., &c,

Service is held in the old Church...Masses on Sundays and Holydays at 7 and 10 1/2 A.M., at which instructions are given. The very few Catholics in the counties of Richmond, Burke, and Columbia, are attended from this place. A Sunday School is taught every Sunday, from 9 until 10 1/2 A.M. Between 60 and 100 children attend thereat.

instNote: ﻿A new Church has been built here.

﻿Masses on Sundays and Holydays at 7 and 10 1/2 A.M. Vespers at 7 P.M. on the same days. Catechism at 4 P.M. Sermons at 10 1/2 A.M., and after Vespers, at which many non-Catholics attend. Stations attended from Macon four times each year are: Milledgeville, Baldwin Co.; Eatenton, Putnam Co.; Irwington, Wilkinson Co.; Americus and Albany. In the latter place, a Church is about to be built.
